what: Row Sums with Optional Minimum Valid Values — sum_n
title: Row Sums with Optional Minimum Valid Values — sum_n
text: sum_n() computes row sums data.frame matrix, handling missing values (NAs) automatically. Row-wise sums calculated across selected numeric columns, optional condition minimum number (proportion) valid (non-missing) values required row included. Non-numeric columns excluded automatically reported.

title: Row Sums with Optional Minimum Valid Values — sum_n
code: sum_n(   data = NULL,   select = dplyr::everything(),   exclude = NULL,   min_valid = NULL,   digits = NULL,   regex = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Row Sums with Optional Minimum Valid Values — sum_n
text: data data.frame matrix. select Columns include. regex = FALSE, use tidyselect syntax (default: dplyr::everything()). regex = TRUE, provide regular expression pattern (character string). exclude Columns exclude (default: NULL). min_valid Minimum number valid (non-NA) values required per row. proportion, applied number selected columns. Defaults NULL (values must valid). digits Optional number decimal places round result. Defaults NULL (rounding). regex Logical. FALSE (default), uses tidyselect helpers. TRUE, select argument treated regular expression. verbose Logical. FALSE (default), messages suppressed. TRUE, prints message non-numeric columns excluded.

title: Row Sums with Optional Minimum Valid Values — sum_n
text: numeric vector row-wise sums

title: Row Sums with Optional Minimum Valid Values — sum_n
code: library(dplyr)  # Create a simple numeric data frame df <- tibble(   var1 = c(10, NA, 30, 40, 50),   var2 = c(5, NA, 15, NA, 25),   var3 = c(NA, 30, 20, 50, 10) )  # Compute row-wise sums (all values must be valid by default) sum_n(df) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Require at least 2 valid (non-NA) values per row sum_n(df, min_valid = 2) #> [1] 15 NA 65 90 85  # Require at least 50% valid (non-NA) values per row sum_n(df, min_valid = 0.5) #> [1] 15 NA 65 90 85  # Round the results to 1 decimal sum_n(df, digits = 1) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Select specific columns sum_n(df, select = c(var1, var2)) #> [1] 15 NA 45 NA 75  # Select specific columns using a pipe df |>   select(var1, var2) |>   sum_n() #> [1] 15 NA 45 NA 75  # Exclude a column sum_n(df, exclude = "var3") #> [1] 15 NA 45 NA 75  # Select columns ending with "1" sum_n(df, select = ends_with("1")) #> [1] 10 NA 30 40 50  # Use with native pipe df |> sum_n(select = starts_with("var")) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Use inside dplyr::mutate() df |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(min_valid = 2)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>    var1  var2  var3 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA        15 #> 2    NA    NA    30        NA #> 3    30    15    20        65 #> 4    40    NA    50        90 #> 5    50    25    10        85  # Select columns directly inside mutate() df |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(select = c(var1, var2), min_valid = 1)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>    var1  var2  var3 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA        15 #> 2    NA    NA    30        NA #> 3    30    15    20        45 #> 4    40    NA    50        40 #> 5    50    25    10        75  # Select columns before mutate df |>   select(var1, var2) |>   mutate(sum_score = sum_n(min_valid = 1)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>    var1  var2 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5        15 #> 2    NA    NA        NA #> 3    30    15        45 #> 4    40    NA        40 #> 5    50    25        75  # Show verbose message df |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(min_valid = 2, digits = 1, verbose = TRUE)) #> sum_n(): Row sums computed with min_valid = 2, regex = FALSE #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>    var1  var2  var3 sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA        15 #> 2    NA    NA    30        NA #> 3    30    15    20        65 #> 4    40    NA    50        90 #> 5    50    25    10        85  # Add character and grouping columns df_mixed <- mutate(df,   name = letters[1:5],   group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "A") ) df_mixed #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>    var1  var2  var3 name  group #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr> #> 1    10     5    NA a     A     #> 2    NA    NA    30 b     A     #> 3    30    15    20 c     B     #> 4    40    NA    50 d     B     #> 5    50    25    10 e     A      # Non-numeric columns are ignored sum_n(df_mixed) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Use inside mutate with mixed data df_mixed |> mutate(sum_score = sum_n(select = starts_with("var"))) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>    var1  var2  var3 name  group sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA a     A            NA #> 2    NA    NA    30 b     A            NA #> 3    30    15    20 c     B            65 #> 4    40    NA    50 d     B            NA #> 5    50    25    10 e     A            85  # Use everything(), but exclude known non-numeric sum_n(df_mixed, select = everything(), exclude = "group") #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Select columns using regex sum_n(df_mixed, select = "^var", regex = TRUE) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85 sum_n(df_mixed, select = "ar", regex = TRUE) #> [1] NA NA 65 NA 85  # Apply to a subset of rows df_mixed[1:3, ] |> sum_n(select = starts_with("var")) #> [1] NA NA 65  # Store the result in a new column df_mixed$sum_score <- sum_n(df_mixed, select = starts_with("var")) df_mixed #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>    var1  var2  var3 name  group sum_score #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1    10     5    NA a     A            NA #> 2    NA    NA    30 b     A            NA #> 3    30    15    20 c     B            65 #> 4    40    NA    50 d     B            NA #> 5    50    25    10 e     A            85  # With a numeric matrix mat <- matrix(c(1, 2, NA, 4, 5, NA, 7, 8, 9), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) mat #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    1    2   NA #> [2,]    4    5   NA #> [3,]    7    8    9 mat |> sum_n(min_valid = 2) #> [1]  3  9 24

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: Builds publication-ready frequency cross-tabulation table one many categorical variables selected tidyselect syntax. , produces grouped cross-tabulation summaries (using cross_tab() internally) Chi-squared p-values optional association measures. Without , produces one-way frequency-style summaries. Multiple output formats available via output: printed ASCII table ("default"), wide long numeric data.frame ("data.frame", "long"), publication-ready tables ("tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel", "clipboard", "word").

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
code: table_categorical(   data,   select,   by = NULL,   labels = NULL,   levels_keep = NULL,   include_total = TRUE,   drop_na = TRUE,   weights = NULL,   rescale = FALSE,   correct = FALSE,   simulate_p = FALSE,   simulate_B = 2000,   percent_digits = 1,   p_digits = 3,   v_digits = 2,   assoc_measure = "auto",   assoc_ci = FALSE,   decimal_mark = ".",   output = c("default", "data.frame", "long", "tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel",     "clipboard", "word"),   indent_text = "  ",   indent_text_excel_clipboard = strrep(" ", 6),   add_multilevel_header = TRUE,   blank_na_wide = FALSE,   excel_path = NULL,   excel_sheet = "Categorical",   clipboard_delim = "\t",   word_path = NULL )

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: data data frame. select Columns include row variables. Supports tidyselect syntax character vectors column names. Optional grouping column used columns/groups. Accepts unquoted column name single character column name. labels optional character vector display labels variables named select (must length order). NULL (default), column names used -. levels_keep Optional character vector levels keep/order row modalities. NULL, observed levels kept. include_total Logical. TRUE (default), includes Total group available. drop_na Logical. TRUE (default), removes rows NA row/group variable cross-tabulation. FALSE, missing values displayed dedicated "(Missing)" level. weights Optional weights. Either NULL (default), numeric vector length nrow(data), single column data supplied unquoted name character string. rescale Logical. FALSE (default), weights used -. TRUE, rescales weights total weighted N matches raw N. Passed spicy::cross_tab(). correct Logical. FALSE (default), continuity correction applied. TRUE, applies Yates correction 2x2 chi-squared contexts. Passed spicy::cross_tab(). simulate_p Logical. FALSE (default), uses asymptotic p-values. TRUE, uses Monte Carlo simulation. Passed spicy::cross_tab(). simulate_B Integer. Number Monte Carlo replicates simulate_p = TRUE. Defaults 2000. percent_digits Number digits percentages report outputs. Defaults 1. p_digits Number digits p-values (except < .001). Defaults 3. v_digits Number digits association measure. Defaults 2. assoc_measure Passed cross_tab(). association measure report ("auto", "cramer_v", "phi", "gamma", "tau_b", "tau_c", "somers_d", "lambda", "none"). Defaults "auto". assoc_ci Passed cross_tab(). TRUE, includes confidence interval association measure. data formats ("data.frame", "long", "excel", "clipboard"), two extra columns CI lower CI upper added. rendered formats ("gt", "tinytable", "flextable", "word"), CI shown inline (e.g., .14 [.08, .19]). Defaults FALSE. decimal_mark Decimal separator ("." ","). Defaults ".". output Output format. One : "default" (printed ASCII table, returned invisibly) "data.frame" (wide numeric data.frame) "long" (long numeric data.frame) "tinytable" (requires tinytable) "gt" (requires gt) "flextable" (requires flextable) "excel" (requires openxlsx2) "clipboard" (requires clipr) "word" (requires flextable officer) indent_text Prefix used modality labels report table building. Defaults "  " (two spaces). indent_text_excel_clipboard Stronger indentation used Excel clipboard exports. Defaults six non-breaking spaces. add_multilevel_header Logical. TRUE (default), merges top headers Excel export. blank_na_wide Logical. FALSE (default), NA values kept -wide raw output. TRUE, replaces empty strings. excel_path Path output = "excel". Defaults NULL. excel_sheet Sheet name Excel export. Defaults "Categorical". clipboard_delim Delimiter clipboard text export. Defaults "\t". word_path Path output = "word" optional save path output = "flextable". Defaults NULL.

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: Depends output: "default": prints styled ASCII table returns underlying data.frame invisibly (S3 class "spicy_categorical_table"). "data.frame": wide data.frame one row per variable–level combination. used, columns Variable, Level, one pair n / \% columns per group level (plus Total include_total = TRUE), followed Chi2, df, p, association measure column. = NULL, columns Variable, Level, n, \%. "long": long data.frame columns variable, level, group, n, percent (chi2, df, p, association measure columns used). "tinytable": tinytable object. "gt": gt_tbl object. "flextable": flextable object. "excel" / "word": writes disk returns file path invisibly. "clipboard": copies table returns display data.frame invisibly.

title: Categorical summary table — table_categorical
text: used, selected variable cross-tabulated grouping variable cross_tab(). Chi-squared statistics, p-values, chosen association measure reported variable. Optional output engines require suggested packages: tinytable output = "tinytable" gt output = "gt" flextable output = "flextable" flextable + officer output = "word" openxlsx2 output = "excel" clipr output = "clipboard"

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: Computes descriptive statistics (mean, SD, min, max, confidence interval mean, n) one many continuous variables selected tidyselect syntax. , produces grouped summaries optional group-comparison tests (test), p-values (p_value), test statistics (statistic), effect sizes (effect_size / effect_size_ci). Without , produces one-way descriptive summaries. Multiple output formats available via output: printed ASCII table ("default"), plain numeric data.frame ("data.frame"), publication-ready tables ("tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel", "clipboard", "word").

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
code: table_continuous(   data,   select = dplyr::everything(),   by = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   regex = FALSE,   test = c("welch", "student", "nonparametric"),   p_value = FALSE,   statistic = FALSE,   effect_size = FALSE,   effect_size_ci = FALSE,   labels = NULL,   ci_level = 0.95,   digits = 2,   decimal_mark = ".",   output = c("default", "data.frame", "tinytable", "gt", "flextable", "excel",     "clipboard", "word"),   excel_path = NULL,   excel_sheet = "Descriptives",   clipboard_delim = "\t",   word_path = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: data data.frame. select Columns include. regex = FALSE, use tidyselect syntax character vector column names (default: dplyr::everything()). regex = TRUE, provide regular expression pattern (character string). Optional grouping column. Accepts unquoted column name single character column name. column need numeric. exclude Columns exclude. Supports tidyselect syntax character vectors column names. regex Logical. FALSE (default), uses tidyselect helpers. TRUE, select argument treated regular expression. test Character. Statistical test use comparing groups. One "welch" (default), "student", "nonparametric". "welch": Welch t-test (2 groups) Welch one-way ANOVA (3+ groups). assume equal variances. "student": Student t-test (2 groups) classic one-way ANOVA (3+ groups). Assumes equal variances. "nonparametric": Wilcoxon rank-sum / Mann–Whitney U (2 groups) Kruskal–Wallis H (3+ groups). Used supplied together p_value = TRUE, statistic = TRUE, effect_size = TRUE. Ignored otherwise. p_value Logical. TRUE used, adds p-value column test specified test. Defaults FALSE. Ignored used. statistic Logical. TRUE used, test statistic shown additional column (e.g., t(df) = ..., F(df1, df2) = ..., W = ..., H(df) = ...). p_value statistic independent; either can enabled. Defaults FALSE. Ignored used. effect_size Logical. TRUE used, adds effect-size column ("ES"). measure chosen automatically: Hedges' g (bias-corrected) - 2 groups, parametric (CI via Hedges & Olkin approximation). Eta-squared (\(\eta^2\)) - 3+ groups, parametric (CI via noncentral F distribution). Rank-biserial r (r_rb) - 2 groups, nonparametric (CI via Fisher z-transform). Epsilon-squared (\(\varepsilon^2\)) - 3+ groups, nonparametric (CI via percentile bootstrap, 2 000 replicates). Defaults FALSE. Ignored used. effect_size_ci Logical. TRUE, appends confidence interval effect size brackets (e.g., g = 0.45 [0.22, 0.68]). Implies effect_size = TRUE. Defaults FALSE. labels optional named character vector variable labels. Names must match column names data. NULL (default), labels auto-detected variable attributes (e.g., haven labels); none found, column name used. ci_level Confidence level mean confidence interval (default: 0.95). Must 0 1 exclusive. digits Number decimal places numeric output (default: 2). decimal_mark Character used decimal separator. Either "." (default) ",". output Output format. One : "default" (printed ASCII table, returned invisibly) "data.frame" (plain numeric data.frame) "tinytable" (requires tinytable) "gt" (requires gt) "flextable" (requires flextable) "excel" (requires openxlsx2) "clipboard" (requires clipr) "word" (requires flextable officer) excel_path File path output = "excel". excel_sheet Sheet name output = "excel" (default: "Descriptives"). clipboard_delim Delimiter output = "clipboard" (default: "\t"). word_path File path output = "word". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints messages excluded non-numeric columns (default: FALSE).

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: Depends output: "default": prints styled ASCII table returns underlying data.frame invisibly (S3 class "spicy_continuous_table"). "data.frame": plain data.frame columns variable, label, group (used), mean, sd, min, max, ci_lower, ci_upper, n. used together p_value = TRUE, statistic = TRUE, effect_size = TRUE, additional columns appended (populated first row variable block ): test_type – test identifier (e.g., "welch_t", "welch_anova", "student_t", "anova", "wilcoxon", "kruskal"). statistic, df1, df2, p.value – test results. es_type – effect-size identifier ("hedges_g", "eta_sq", "r_rb", "epsilon_sq"), effect_size = TRUE. es_value, es_ci_lower, es_ci_upper – effect-size estimate confidence interval bounds. "tinytable": tinytable object. "gt": gt_tbl object. "flextable": flextable object. "excel" / "word": writes disk returns file path invisibly. "clipboard": copies table returns display data.frame invisibly.

title: Continuous summary table — table_continuous
text: Non-numeric columns silently dropped (set verbose = TRUE see columns excluded). single constant column passed, SD CI shown "--" ASCII table. Optional output engines require suggested packages: tinytable output = "tinytable" gt output = "gt" flextable output = "flextable" flextable + officer output = "word" openxlsx2 output = "excel" clipr output = "clipboard"

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: uncertainty_coef() computes Uncertainty Coefficient (Theil's U) two-way contingency table, based information entropy.

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
code: uncertainty_coef(   x,   direction = c("symmetric", "row", "column"),   detail = FALSE,   conf_level = 0.95,   digits = 3L,   .include_se = FALSE )

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: x contingency table (class table). direction Direction prediction: "symmetric" (default), "row" (column predicts row), "column" (row predicts column). detail Logical. FALSE (default), return estimate numeric scalar. TRUE, return named numeric vector including confidence interval p-value. conf_level number 0 1 giving confidence level (default 0.95). used detail = TRUE. Set NULL omit confidence interval. digits Number decimal places used printing result (default 3). affects detail = TRUE output. .include_se Internal parameter; use.

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: structure cramer_v(): scalar detail = FALSE, named vector detail = TRUE. p-value tests H0: U = 0 (Wald z-test).

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: uncertainty coefficient measures association using Shannon entropy. direction = "row": \(U = (H_X + H_Y - H_{XY}) / H_X\), \(H_X\), \(H_Y\) marginal entropies \(H_{XY}\) joint entropy. symmetric version \(U = 2 (H_X + H_Y - H_{XY}) / (H_X + H_Y)\). Standard error formulas follow DescTools implementations (Signorell et al., 2024); see cramer_v() full references.

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
code: tab <- table(sochealth$smoking, sochealth$education) uncertainty_coef(tab) #> [1] 0.01148762 uncertainty_coef(tab, direction = "row", detail = TRUE) #> Estimate  CI lower  CI upper      p #>    0.018     0.003     0.032  0.021

what: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
text: varlist() lists variables data frame extracts essential metadata, including variable names, labels, summary values, classes, number distinct values, number valid (non-missing) observations, number missing values. vl() convenient shorthand varlist() offers identical functionality shorter name.

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
code: varlist(   x,   ...,   values = FALSE,   tbl = FALSE,   include_na = FALSE,   .raw_expr = substitute(x) )  vl(x, ..., values = FALSE, tbl = FALSE, include_na = FALSE)

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist

Missing values (NA, NaN) optionally appended end (controlled via include_na). Class: class variable (possibly multiple, e.g. "labelled", "numeric") N_distinct: number distinct non-missing values N_valid: number non-missing observations NAs: number missing observations tbl = FALSE used interactively, summary displayed Viewer pane. data frame transformation (e.g. head(df) df[ , 1:3]), asterisk (*) appended name title (e.g. vl: df*).

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: yule_q() computes Yule's Q coefficient association 2x2 contingency table.

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
code: yule_q(x, detail = FALSE, conf_level = 0.95, digits = 3L, .include_se = FALSE)

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: x contingency table (class table). detail Logical. FALSE (default), return estimate numeric scalar. TRUE, return named numeric vector including confidence interval p-value. conf_level number 0 1 giving confidence level (default 0.95). used detail = TRUE. Set NULL omit confidence interval. digits Number decimal places used printing result (default 3). affects detail = TRUE output. .include_se Internal parameter; use.

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: structure cramer_v(): scalar detail = FALSE, named vector detail = TRUE. p-value tests H0: Q = 0 (Wald z-test).

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: 2x2 table cells \(, b, c, d\), Yule's Q \(Q = (ad - bc) / (ad + bc)\). equivalent Goodman-Kruskal Gamma 2x2 tables. asymptotic standard error \(SE = 0.5 (1 - Q^2) \sqrt{1/+ 1/b + 1/c + 1/d}\). Standard error formulas follow DescTools implementations (Signorell et al., 2024); see cramer_v() full references.
